extraction.article_title: Hackers Exploit React2Shell in automated credential theft campaign

ttps.description: Exploit public-facing application to gain initial access through a vulnerable Next.js/React server-side component.
Unsecured credentials harvesting from environment variables, config files, SSH keys, and cloud tokens after application compromise.
Data from local system or application directories is collected for exfiltration, including secrets and key material.
Credential theft includes SSH private keys that can enable remote access over SSH.

attack_chain: Automated scanning for exposed vulnerable Next.js applications
Exploitation of React2Shell to execute attacker-controlled logic on the target
Deployment of a temporary script into a standard temp directory
Harvesting of secrets from environment variables, application configs, and cloud/SSH credential stores
Exfiltration of collected database credentials, API keys, cloud tokens, and private keys

proposed_vectors.name_en: Next.js server-side component exploit leading to secret theft
proposed_vectors.name_ko: Next.js 서버 측 컴포넌트 취약점 악용으로 인한 비밀정보 탈취

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: A vulnerable server-side Next.js/React component can be coerced into executing attacker-supplied code or server-side actions, which then allows a follow-on script to enumerate local secrets and credentials.
proposed_kb_patterns.payload:
POST /api/action HTTP/1.1
Host: target
Content-Type: application/json

{"action":"__proto__","command":"id > /tmp/react2shell_test && env | head -n 20 > /tmp/react2shell_env"}
detect: Response or side effects indicating command execution
New log entries showing env enumeration or child process execution

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: A server-side flaw can be used to make the application fetch attacker-chosen internal URLs, such as cloud metadata endpoints, revealing temporary cloud credentials or instance identity data.
detect: Response contains instance profile or role name

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: If the compromised app or a post-exploitation script reads files using unsanitized paths, traversal sequences can force disclosure of local configuration files that contain secrets.
detect: Disclosure of environment variable names or values
File-not-found behavior changes to successful file read

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: If server-side authorization checks on privileged routes are weak or trust client-side state, crafted requests may directly access admin or internal endpoints used for secret collection or task execution.
detect: Admin data returned without valid session
Privilege-related response content appears to unauthenticated users
Audit logs show access to internal endpoints from non-admin sessions

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: If the secret-harvesting web app exposes per-host or per-job records with predictable identifiers, an attacker can enumerate other victims' harvested data or job status by changing object IDs.
detect: Access to records belonging to a different host or tenant
